title: Acantholimon creticum
taxonomy: Acantholimon creticum is classified as a species within the plant family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Acantholimon, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as limonium or plumbago. Acantholimon creticum is native to the Mediterranean region, where it thrives in dry, rocky areas. This plant is often characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and narrow, linear leaves. Its classification is further supported by its position within the family Plumbaginaceae, which includes other flowering plants such as Plumbago and Armeria.

intro: Acantholimon creticum, also known as Cretan Acantholimon, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to the treatment of various ailments such as wound healing and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Acantholimon creticum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
